data.epi_content:
As usual, while Lydia was putting a headband on me and I was letting her insults go in one ear and out the other, she said something I couldn't ignore.

"Miss, aren't you getting tired of just wearing headbands all the time?"

To be honest, it wasn't so much that I couldn't ignore her words, but rather that the object in her hands demanded my full attention.

"I'm not tired of it at all. I feel refreshed every time I wear a headband."

What I meant was that I feel a fresh sense of humiliation each time.

So, I'd really appreciate it if you'd put away that thing you're holding.

"Well, if that's how you feel, I won't argue...

But I'm getting tired of watching you tremble while writing.

So, I prepared something you'll hate even more this time."

It was a dog collar.

And not just any collar—one with spikes all around it like those worn by bulldogs, something anyone would immediately recognize as a dog collar. A monstrosity.

"I'm going to have lunch. While I'm gone, wear this and wait for me."

"...This?"

"Why are you looking at me like that?

What kind of person would take a dog for a walk without a leash?

You should be grateful I'm letting you wear clothes at all."

Lydia fastened the collar around my neck as I sat there, my body trembling slightly. Despite it clearly being a dog collar, she had attached a bell to it.

Then she stared at my trembling figure for a long while before frowning.

I thought she might hit me, but surely not.

Though "surely not" has been the death of many before.

"Leash, collar. Hmm. Not as satisfying as I expected.

You there, could you lend me your gloves? Our miss here has cold hands."

She snatched gloves from a student whose name I didn't even know and put them on my hands.

"Well, fingers for a dog just doesn't make sense! Stay like this, uncomfortable as it may be.

I'll feed you when I get back."

Then she extended the leash and tied it loosely to the protruding lock hole of a locker.

If it came undone, what would Lydia do? Would she slap me?

Though she's nothing special, I'm even less so. I swallowed the self-loathing that washed over me again at the realization that I could do nothing, and gently closed my eyes.

"Feed...?"

"Yes, your feed. They say people in slums eat garbage for meals.

But since you're my dog, I'll give you food so good people will say you're living the high life!"

After putting the gloves on me, she kneaded my arms.

It stung a bit as my wounds opened, but I didn't show it.

Looking ridiculous, perhaps even cute to someone who didn't know the situation, I just sat in the chair.

With slightly dazed eyes.

When my mind gets tired, rather than my vision blurring, I start to lose focus while staring ahead.

Then everything blurs, and all the lingering images around me seem to mock me.

As they passed by the classroom door, some students laughed at me, while others looked at me with pitying eyes.

The pity hurt more than the mockery.

Fortunately, not many people tried to bully me.

After all, from others' perspectives, I've lived a good life.

Regardless of someone's status, I always used polite speech and never acted contrary to proper etiquette.

And that's quite remarkable in this world where status seems to determine everything.

Though in reality, this world exists for Vivian more than for status.

But somehow, I encountered myself while experiencing this absurd, torturous, cursed situation.

It was confusing at first, but it's become natural as time passed.
data.epi_content2:
This young lady, who always maintained etiquette and respect for others, but whose inner self was terribly arrogant and looked down on everyone, lost everything that had supported that attitude.

Family, relatives, loved ones, servants, wealth, power, status.

All of it disappeared in an instant.

But now, this pitiful young lady tries to preserve the foundations that once defined her.

Even if it means taking her own life because she can no longer endure.

Taking one's life was something even a girl in her formative years could accomplish.

If only she could find a proper justification and excuse to escape.

I've always been one to run and hide, so I just went along with whatever this young lady wanted to do.

But to die and not die?

To experience that unbearable pain twice, with no reward?

It was absurd.

At least for this young lady.

When you accomplish something, others praise you.

When you do what's expected, subordinates look at you with reverence.

Everyone was busy admiring, loving, and respecting me.

Of course, I knew most of that came from my family name, but such a life is enough to make anyone addicted to that reward psychology.

So, it's a bit sad to meet Evan while wearing a dog collar, a headband with puppy ears, and mittens, all in an attempt to somehow survive.

"Erica, why are you like this...?"

"You can see, I'm being bullied."

"By whom?"

"Why, are you going to stop it?"

"Yes."

"Why would you? We're nothing to each other.

Don't make things worse by trying to do something.

This is quite gentlemanly, actually."

Much better than being locked in a locker for hours, unable to move.

"Don't untie that. She'll make a scene when she comes back."

"What right does a mere baron's daughter have to do this!?"

I'm even less than that.

The reason no one bothers Vivian—or fails when they try—is because she's always surrounded by people.

Whether it's the Crown Prince, you, or some unnamed northern duke—it would be funny if his name was Lee Seong-gye.

Anyway, such high and mighty people constantly try to court her without rest.

But me?

You, Vivian, and occasionally a maid who comes to clean. That's it.

No one else.

I can't stand the sight of Vivian, but she brings me decent food and is the only one who shows any concern.

And you, you're so half-hearted about everything.

You feel sorry for me, yet you're so infatuated with Vivian that you use me, or magic, as an excuse to always be by her side.

But as Vivian herself said before, she's not interested in love.

Perhaps the protagonist will be someone who makes her interested, but that doesn't seem decided yet.

Everyone, including me, is just a supporting character.

Thinking this, I speak in a flat voice filled with slight reproach, resentment, and desperation, cursing my trembling vocal cords.

"I'm... I'm worse off than that baron's daughter right now."

My voice trembled uncontrollably, causing my pronunciation to slip.
data.epi_content3:
If the atmosphere weren't like this, I might have managed a small, meaningless laugh.

Evan's expression was just trembling like my body.

He probably didn't know what to say, and I didn't look like myself, so he must have been confused.

"Look at me. Who could possibly look at me now and remember who I used to be?

Who? No one... except you and Vivian.

No one visits me except you two."

"So you're going to keep wearing that ridiculous dog collar?"

"Yes, maybe you would rather die in this situation.

But I have too strong an attachment to life."

Actually, it's that I can't die.

More precisely, I can die, but the terrible pain experienced while dying is too cursed.

I don't even know myself anymore.

What I want to do, what I want to eat, what I truly desire.

Things like that.

I'm not some adolescent girl, so this search for self-identity is ridiculous.

Oh, but I am an adolescent girl.

I don't hear a voice correcting me.

"By the way, Evan, do you like me?"

He makes a subtle expression.

Slightly biting his lip.

"...Yes, we're childhood friends."

Right, I don't even have human goodwill left for you.

"So you do like me. Well, familiarity and lovability are different, and liking and loving are also different words. Though they sound similar.

Thanks to that, you can vaguely dodge difficult questions."

So to prevent him from dodging vaguely, I press him a little.

In my current state, I can't even think properly, but I speak as if venting, relying on old affection.

"Then will you marry me?"

Ha, not a chance.

Evan bites his lip.

But it wasn't so troubling that his teeth drew blood from where they touched.

When I bite my lip, blood flows immediately.

Maybe even my lips are weak.

Anyway, Evan couldn't answer and just kept his mouth shut.

"......"

Finally, he makes a gloomy expression that might be mistaken for crying.

"You won't, right? Then there's no solution.

Unless Vivian gets taken by some other man.

Right now, I'm worse off than most commoners."

If I had been born a commoner from the start, it might be different, but when you fall from a high place to beside commoners, you can't even fit in with them.

Far from receiving admiring glances, you get looks of contempt for not being able to keep your place.

"Like in those cheap novels sold in the marketplace, the only way for a worthless woman to become nobility is to catch a handsome noble man and marry him."

So I pushed Evan away with both hands as he tried to untie the leash from the locker.

Not that he would fall backward from that.

"So don't try to do anything.

This is enough, what more could I want?

It's not like I'm dying or anything. Heh."
